evidence: .husky/ directory present with commit-msg, pre-commit, pre-push hooks (.husky/commit-msg, .husky/pre-commit, .husky/pre-push)
package.json scripts.prepare = "husky"
husky@^9.1.7 devDependency
gates.expectation: Husky or Lefthook is configured for the repository.
gates.gap: None.
gates.remediation: No action — husky is installed and wired via the prepare script.

evidence: @biomejs/biome@^1.9.4 devDependency
biome.json formatter.enabled = true
.husky/pre-commit line 12 runs `pnpm exec biome check --staged --no-errors-on-unmatched .` (check includes the formatter)
NO lint-staged configuration block in package.json and no .lintstagedrc file anywhere in the repo
gates.expectation: Prettier or Biome formats staged files via lint-staged.
gates.gap: Formatting of staged files is achieved through `biome check --staged` invoked directly in the pre-commit hook, not through a lint-staged configuration block. The baseline signal `lint-staged configuration block` does not resolve, so the gate is graded misconfigured per the strict baseline. `biome check` reports formatting issues but, without `--write`/`--fix`, does not auto-apply them on commit. This is a deliberate, documented deviation, not an oversight.
gates.remediation: Accept the direct `biome check --staged` approach as an intentional deviation (Biome's native --staged supersedes lint-staged), or add a lint-staged block mapping staged files to `biome check --write --no-errors-on-unmatched` so formatting is applied rather than only reported.

evidence: @biomejs/biome@^1.9.4 devDependency
biome.json linter.enabled = true (recommended rules + noUnusedImports / noUnusedVariables)
.husky/pre-commit line 12 runs `pnpm exec biome check --staged ...` (check includes the linter)
NO lint-staged block invoking the linter
gates.expectation: ESLint or Biome lints staged files via lint-staged, with --fix allowed.
gates.gap: Linting of staged files runs via `biome check --staged` directly in the hook rather than through a lint-staged block, so the baseline's `lint-staged block invoking it` signal does not resolve. The lint DOES run on staged files only (good), but `--write`/`--fix` is not passed, so fixable lint issues are reported, not auto-applied. Deliberate deviation.
gates.remediation: Treat the direct `biome check --staged` invocation as an intentional, simpler-than-lint-staged design, or migrate to a lint-staged block with `biome check --write` to allow safe auto-fixes.

evidence: typescript@^5.7.2 devDependency
tsconfig.json present
.husky/pre-commit line 9 runs `pnpm typecheck` which is `tsc --noEmit` (package.json scripts.typecheck)
The type check runs over the ENTIRE project on every commit, not scoped to staged TypeScript files (no tsc-files / no staged scoping)
gates.expectation: tsc --noEmit (or equivalent) runs against staged TypeScript files.
gates.gap: A type check runs on every commit, which is stronger than nothing — but it is a full-project `tsc --noEmit`, not a staged-file-scoped check. The baseline's staged-scoping signal (often via tsc-files) does not resolve, so this is misconfigured rather than present. On large projects this makes the commit loop slower than a staged-only check would; here it is a deliberate full-safety trade-off.
gates.remediation: Accept the full `tsc --noEmit` on pre-commit as the stricter, simpler choice (recommended for a small template), or introduce `tsc-files` to scope the type check to staged files for a faster commit loop.

evidence: @commitlint/cli@^19.6.1, @commitlint/config-conventional@^19.6.0, @commitlint/types@^19.5.0 devDependencies
commitlint.config.ts present, extends @commitlint/config-conventional with a curated type-enum and subject-case rules
.husky/commit-msg runs `pnpm exec commitlint --edit "$1"`
gates.expectation: commitlint enforces Conventional Commits on the subject line.
gates.gap: None.
gates.remediation: No action — commitlint is installed, configured, and wired into the commit-msg hook.

evidence: .husky/pre-commit line 18 runs `pnpm secret-scan`
package.json scripts.secret-scan = "bash scripts/secret-scan.sh"
scripts/secret-scan.sh runs `gitleaks protect --staged --redact --no-banner` when gitleaks is installed (conditional on `command -v gitleaks`)
CI backstop: .github/workflows/ci.yml security job runs gitleaks/gitleaks-action@v3 over full history unconditionally
gates.expectation: gitleaks (protect) or detect-secrets runs against staged content before commit.
gates.gap: The local scan is conditional — `gitleaks protect --staged` runs only when the gitleaks binary is present, otherwise it prints install instructions and exits 0. This is an intentional design (gitleaks is a native binary, not an npm dep) and the gate's intent — staged-content secret scanning wired into pre-commit — is satisfied, with CI providing an unconditional hard backstop. Signals resolve, so graded present. NOTE: a developer without gitleaks installed gets no local enforcement until CI.
gates.remediation: No blocking action. To remove the soft spot, add gitleaks to the bootstrap (`pnpm setup:agents`) or document `brew install gitleaks` as a required step so the local staged scan is never silently skipped.

evidence: .husky/pre-push present and executable
Wired into husky via the prepare script and the .husky/_ runtime
gates.expectation: A pre-push hook exists and is wired into the hook runner.
gates.gap: None.
gates.remediation: No action — the pre-push hook exists and is active.

evidence: .husky/pre-push runs ONLY: `pnpm test:coverage` (line 9), `pnpm build` (line 12), `pnpm e2e` (line 15)
No `pnpm typecheck` / `tsc --noEmit` invocation in .husky/pre-push
Type checking is delegated to the pre-commit hook (full `tsc --noEmit` there) and re-run in CI (ci.yml quality job)
gates.expectation: tsc --noEmit runs over the entire project on pre-push.
gates.gap: The pre-push hook does not run a type check. The repo deliberately splits responsibilities — full type check lives on pre-commit, behaviour suites on pre-push — so a contributor who edits a type after their last commit and pushes without re-committing would not get a pre-push type check (CI still catches it). Per the baseline this signal does not resolve at the pre-push stage, so it is missing here.
gates.remediation: If you want pre-push to be a complete local mirror of CI, add `pnpm typecheck` to .husky/pre-push before the test step. Otherwise document that type safety is owned by pre-commit + CI by design.

evidence: .husky/pre-push contains no `biome check` / `biome lint` / `eslint .` invocation
Full lint runs on pre-commit (`biome check --staged` is staged-scoped) and in CI (ci.yml quality job runs `pnpm check` = `biome check .` whole-repo)
gates.expectation: The project's lint command runs the whole codebase on pre-push and fails on warnings.
gates.gap: No whole-codebase lint runs at the pre-push stage. The only local lint is the staged-scoped `biome check --staged` on pre-commit; a whole-repo `biome check .` runs only in CI. The baseline's pre-push full-lint signal does not resolve, so this is missing.
gates.remediation: Add `pnpm check` (`biome check .`) to .husky/pre-push for a whole-repo lint before pushing, or accept that the whole-repo lint is owned by CI by design.

evidence: .husky/pre-push line 9 runs `pnpm test:coverage`
package.json scripts.test:coverage = "jest --coverage"
jest.config.ts present with coverageThreshold global 80% across branches/functions/lines/statements
gates.expectation: Vitest or Jest runs the unit-test suite to completion on pre-push.
gates.gap: None.
gates.remediation: No action — the Jest suite (with coverage) runs on every push.

evidence: .husky/pre-push line 12 runs `pnpm build`
package.json scripts.build = "vite build" (Vite production build)
gates.expectation: The production build command runs and exits cleanly on pre-push.
gates.gap: None.
gates.remediation: No action — the Vite production build runs as a pre-push smoke test.

evidence: .github/workflows/ci.yml present (quality, fallow, test, build, e2e, security jobs)
Additional workflows: .github/workflows/codeql.yml, .github/workflows/lighthouse.yml, .github/workflows/styleproof.yml, .github/workflows/styleproof-approve.yml
Triggers on push to main and pull_request
gates.expectation: At least one workflow definition exists.
gates.gap: None.
gates.remediation: No action — multiple GitHub Actions workflows are present.

evidence: ci.yml quality job: `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` then `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm check`
ci.yml test job: `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` then `pnpm test:coverage`
ci.yml build job: `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` then `pnpm build` then `pnpm size`
ci.yml e2e job: clean install then `pnpm exec playwright install` then `pnpm e2e`
Each job is a fresh ubuntu-latest checkout with frozen-lockfile install
gates.expectation: The workflow installs dependencies fresh and runs type-check, lint, unit tests, and build.
gates.gap: None. CI is actually a SUPERSET of the local hooks — it additionally runs the whole-repo type check and lint that the pre-push hook delegates to it.
gates.remediation: No action — all pre-push-equivalent gates re-run on a clean checkout, plus the full typecheck/lint.

evidence: @playwright/test@^1.49.1 devDependency
playwright.config.ts present
ci.yml e2e job runs `pnpm exec playwright install --with-deps chromium` then `pnpm e2e`
package.json scripts.e2e = "playwright test e2e/app.spec.ts e2e/accessibility.spec.ts"
gates.expectation: Playwright or Cypress runs against a build of the application.
gates.gap: None.
gates.remediation: No action — Playwright runs in CI against the app.

evidence: jest.config.ts coverageThreshold.global = { branches: 80, functions: 80, lines: 80, statements: 80 }
ci.yml test job runs `pnpm test:coverage` (jest --coverage) which fails when the threshold is not met
Coverage artifact uploaded for inspection
gates.expectation: The test runner is configured with a coverage threshold and the workflow fails when it is not met.
gates.gap: None. Worth noting collectCoverageFrom excludes index.ts barrels, main.tsx and App.tsx (composition root, covered by E2E) — feature logic is in scope.
gates.remediation: No action — coverage threshold is configured and enforced in CI.

evidence: size-limit@^12.1.0 and @size-limit/file@^12.1.0 devDependencies
.size-limit.json present: app JS budget 80 kB, CSS budget 5 kB against dist/assets/*
package.json scripts.size = "size-limit"
ci.yml build job step `Bundle-size budget` runs `pnpm size` after `pnpm build` (fails the job when the budget is exceeded)
gates.expectation: A bundle-size check runs and fails when the budget is exceeded.
gates.gap: None.
gates.remediation: No action — size-limit enforces a JS + CSS budget in CI.

evidence: @axe-core/playwright@^4.11.3 devDependency; e2e/accessibility.spec.ts uses AxeBuilder with wcag2a/wcag2aa/wcag21a/wcag21aa tags and asserts zero violations
ci.yml e2e job runs `pnpm e2e`, which includes e2e/accessibility.spec.ts (real Chromium axe scan)

jest-axe@^10.0.0 devDependency; jest.setup.ts extends expect with toHaveNoViolations; src/features/threshold-counter/ThresholdCounter.a11y.test.tsx runs a component-level axe check (re-run in CI test job via pnpm test:coverage)
Lighthouse CI also asserts categories:accessibility minScore 0.9 as a hard error (lighthouserc.json)
gates.expectation: Automated accessibility checks run against components or pages.
gates.gap: None. Accessibility is covered at three layers: jsdom component axe (jest-axe), real-browser page axe (@axe-core/playwright), and Lighthouse a11y budget.
gates.remediation: No action — automated accessibility checks run in CI at multiple layers.

evidence: ci.yml security job runs `pnpm audit --audit-level=high` against the lockfile
ci.yml security job runs gitleaks/gitleaks-action@v3 (secret scan over full history)
.github/dependabot.yml configures weekly npm + github-actions dependency updates (open-pull-requests-limit 10, dev deps grouped)
gates.expectation: A vulnerability scanner runs against the lockfile.
gates.gap: None. Both an active audit (pnpm audit) and a passive update channel (Dependabot) are in place.
gates.remediation: No action — dependency vulnerability scanning and automated updates are configured.

evidence: No license-checker, license-compliance, licensee, or @inquirer/license-checker-rs devDependency in package.json
No workflow step in any of .github/workflows/* invoking a license allow-list or deny-list check
gates.expectation: A license check runs and enforces an allow-list or deny-list.
gates.gap: No license-compliance gate exists. Nothing prevents a transitively-introduced copyleft or otherwise-incompatible license from entering the dependency tree. This is the one CI gate from the baseline that remains genuinely absent.
gates.remediation: Add `license-checker-rs` (or `license-checker`) as a devDependency, define an allow-list of acceptable SPDX licenses, and add a CI step (e.g. in the security job) that fails when a dependency falls outside the allow-list.

evidence: @lhci/cli@^0.15.1 devDependency
lighthouserc.json present: staticDistDir ./dist; assertions categories:accessibility >=0.9 (error), performance >=0.8 (warn), best-practices >=0.9 (warn), seo off
.github/workflows/lighthouse.yml runs on pull_request: clean install, `pnpm build`, then `pnpm exec lhci autorun`
package.json scripts.lhci = "lhci autorun"
gates.expectation: Lighthouse CI runs against a deployed or built version of the application and asserts thresholds.
gates.gap: None. Accessibility is a hard error floor; performance/best-practices are advisory warnings by design.
gates.remediation: No action — Lighthouse CI runs on every pull request and asserts thresholds against the production build.
